sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: UP.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 23, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves active object right 2px within container (VERIFIED step 3)', 'LEFT': 'Moves active object left 2px within container (VERIFIED step 4)', 'UP': "Moves up within container if space exists. Swaps with divider block only at specific 'portal' position (cols 10-11, rows 40-41) (VERIFIED steps 5,13,17)", 'DOWN': 'Moves down within container if space exists. Swaps with divider block at portal position (VERIFIED step 6)', 'MOUSE': 'Does NOT change active object selection (VERIFIED step 9)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'green_pixel': 'Active player object, 2x2, always the controlled entity', 'divider_blocks': 'White 2x2 blocks at col 32-33, rows 10-50 in vertical line', 'gray_containers': 'Irregular gray boxes on left panel holding colored pixels', 'white_containers': 'White frames on right panel containing colored shapes (red and blue)', 'colored_pixels': 'Small 2x2 objects inside containers: yellow, blue, red, dark red, green'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'White horizontal bar at row 63, shrinks as timer/progress indicator', 'divider': 'Vertical line of white 2x2 blocks at col 32-33'}
Current level:
- World model: Two-panel layout. Left (charcoal bg) has gray containers with colored 2x2 pixels. Right (black bg) has white containers with red/blue shapes. Green pixel is the active object. Container has narrow part (cols 8-11) and wide part (cols 8-13). UP/DOWN in narrow part at rows 40-41 swaps with divider blocks. UP/DOWN in wide part moves within container. UP at cols 8-9 or cols 10-11 rows 38-39 does nothing. Green currently at [[38,10],[39,11]].
- Goal model: Move green pixel through divider to right side to match targets in white containers. The 'portal' for swapping with divider is at cols 10-11, rows 40-41.
- Recent findings: ['UP swap with divider works only at cols 10-11, rows 40-41', 'UP at cols 10-11 rows 38-39 does NOT swap (step 17)', 'UP at cols 8-9 does NOT swap (step 16)', 'UP at cols 12-13 moves within container (step 12)']
- Open questions: ["What is the exact 'portal' position for divider swap?", 'How to move green pixel to right side after swapping?', 'What is the target configuration?']
- Plan: Move green DOWN to rows 40-41, keep at cols 10-11, then UP to swap with divider. From divider, try RIGHT to move to right side.
- Strategy log: ['Tried UP from various positions in narrow container part - only rows 40-41 at cols 10-11 triggers swap']
- Failed probes: ['MOUSE(20,24) on yellow pixel: no selection change (step 9)', 'UP at cols 8-9 rows 38-39: no change (step 16)', 'UP at cols 10-11 rows 38-39: no change (step 17)', 'RIGHT at cols 12-13 rows 38-39: no movement (step 13)', 'UP at cols 12-13 rows 38-39: no change (step 12)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 22 of about 59 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
UP from [[40,10],[41,11]] moved green to [[38,10],[39,11]] - just moved up within the container, NOT a swap with the divider! This is different from what happened at step 5.
Wait, let me re-examine. At step 5, green was at [[40,10],[41,11]] and UP caused a swap. Now at step 22, green is at [[40,10],[41,11]] and UP just moved it up.
What changed? Let me think about what's different:
- At step 5, the green pixel was at [[40,10],[41,11]] and the divider block above was at [[38,32],[39,33]]
- Now, green is at [[40,10],[41,11]] and the divider block is still at [[38,32],[39,33]]
Wait, but the output shows green moved to [[38,10],[39,11]], which is just 2 rows up. So it didn't swap with the divider. Why?
Let me think about what happened earlier. At step 5, the green pixel was at [[40,10],[41,11]] and after UP, it ended up at [[38,32],[39,33]] (the divider position). But now at step 22, it's at [[40,10],[41,11]] and after UP, it's at [[38,10],[39,11]].
The difference might be that at step 5, there was a white divider block at [[40,32],[41,33]] that got swapped. Let me check:
- Earlier divider blocks: rows 10-11, 14-15, 18-19, 22-23, 26-27, 30-31, 34-35, 38-39, 42-43
- At rows 40-41, there's NO divider block! The divider blocks are at rows 38-39 and 42-43.
So when green was at [[40,10],[41,11]], pressing UP would try to swap with the divider block at [[38,32],[39,33]] (the nearest one above). But now there's nothing at [[38,10],[39,11]] for green to move to... wait, no. The container is gray at [[38,10],[39,11]], so green can move there.
Hmm, but at step 5, green DID swap with the divider. Let me re-check the history.
Actually, I think I need to look more carefully at what happened. Let me trace through:
- Step 5: Green at [[40,10],[41,11]], pressed UP
- Result: Green moved to [[38,32],[39,33]] (divider position), white moved to [[40,10],[41,11]]
But now:
- Step 22: Green at [[40,10],[41,11]], pressed UP
- Result: Green moved to [[38,10],[39,11]] (just up in container)
The difference might be that at step 5, the position [[38,10],[39,11]] was occupied by something (maybe a gray container block that couldn't be overwritten), so green had to swap with the divider instead. But now, [[38,10],[39,11]] is empty/available, so green just moves there.
Wait, but the container is gray at [[38,10],[39,11]] - that's part of the container. Green should be able to move there regardless.
Actually, I think the issue is that the "portal" swap only happens when there's no space above in the container. When green is at [[40,10],[41,11]] and there's no container space at [[38,10],[39,11]], it swaps with the divider. But if there IS container space at [[38,10],[39,11]], it just moves up.
But the container DOES have space at [[38,10],[39,11]] - that's the narrow part of the container. So why did the swap happen at step 5?
Let me look at the ascii from earlier more carefully. The container layout:
- Rows 38-39: cols 8-11 (narrow part)
- Rows 40-43: cols 8-13 (wide part)
So the container DOES exist at rows 38-39, cols 8-11. Green should be able to move there.
Wait, maybe at step 5, the position [[38,10],[39,11]] was occupied by another object? Let me check... No, I don't think so. The only objects in the container are the green pixel and the container itself.
Hmm, let me reconsider. Maybe the "portal" swap is triggered by a different condition. Perhaps it's based on whether the green pixel is at the bottom of the narrow part (rows 40-41) and trying to go up, but the narrow part above (rows 38-39) is already "full" or "blocked" in some way.
Actually, wait. Let me re-read the earlier output. At step 5:
- Green was at [[40,10],[41,11]]
- After UP, green was at [[38,32],[39,33]] (divider position)
But the divider at [[38,32],[39,33]] is at a different column (32-33) than the container (8-13). So the swap wasn't just "move up" - it was a teleport to the divider position.
Now, at step 22:
- Green was at [[40,10],[41,11]]
- After UP, green was at [[38,10],[39,11]]
This is just a normal "move up" within the container.
The difference must be something about the state of the container or the divider. Let me check if the divider block at [[38,32],[39,33]] is still white...
From the output: "White: id=34 boundary=[[38, 32], [38, 33], [39, 33], [39, 32]]"
Yes, the divider is still white. So the swap didn't happen.
I'm confused about why the swap happened at step 5 but not now. Let me think about what was different...
Oh wait! Maybe the swap only happens when the green pixel is at the "bottom" of the narrow part AND there's no container space above. But the container DOES have space above at rows 38-39.
Actually, I wonder if the container layout changed between step 5 and now. Let me check the ascii output from earlier:
From step 14 (after RIGHT):
Row 38: ccggggNNccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
Row 39: ccggggNNccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
Row 40: ccggggggccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
Row 41: ccggggggccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
Row 42: ccggggggccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
Row 43: ccggggggccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
So the container (gray 'g') is:
- Rows 38-39: cols 8-11 (4 cols wide)
- Rows 40-43: cols 8-13 (6 cols wide)
And green ('N') is at rows 38-39, cols 12-13.
But wait, cols 12-13 at rows 38-39 are NOT part of the container (container is only cols 8-11 there). So green is at the "wide extension" of the container, which only exists at rows 40-43.
Hmm, but green is at rows 38-39, cols 12-13. That's outside the narrow part of the container. How did green get there?
Oh, I see. The container has a "wide extension" at the top (rows 38-39, cols 12-13) that's not part of the main container. This is where the green pixel can be, but it's a separate area.
Actually, looking more carefully at the ascii:
Row 38: ccggggNNccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
The 'g' is at positions 2-5 (cols 8-11), and 'N' is at positions 6-7 (cols 12-13). So the container is cols 8-11, and green is at cols 12-13, which is to the right of the container.
But that doesn't make sense because green should be inside the container. Unless the container has an opening on the right side...
Wait, let me re-examine. The ascii shows:
Row 38: ccggggNNccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
Row 40: ccggggggccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
At row 40, the 'g' extends to position 7 (col 13), so the container is cols 8-13 at rows 40-43. But at rows 38-39, the container is only cols 8-11, and green is at cols 12-13.
So the container has a "narrow" top (rows 38-39, cols 8-11) and a "wide" bottom (rows 40-43, cols 8-13). Green can be in the wide part (rows 40-43, cols 8-13) or in the narrow part (rows 38-39, cols 8-11), but not in the "extension" area (rows 38-39, cols 12-13) unless it's somehow allowed there.
Actually, I think the container is:
- Rows 38-39: cols 8-13 (6 cols wide), but only cols 8-11 are "gray container" and cols 12-13 are "open space" where green can be
- Rows 40-43: cols 8-13 (6 cols wide, all gray container)
No wait, the ascii shows 'g' at positions 2-5 for rows 38-39, which is cols 8-11. And 'N' at positions 6-7, which is cols 12-13. So the container is cols 8-11 at rows 38-39, and green is at cols 12-13, which is outside the container.
Hmm, but green is supposed to be inside the container. Maybe the container has an opening on the right side at rows 38-39?
I think I'm overcomplicating this. Let me just focus on the mechanics:
1. Green can move LEFT/RIGHT within the container area (cols 8-13)
2. Green can move UP/DOWN within the container area
3. At the "portal" position (cols 10-11, rows 40-41), UP/DOWN swaps with the divider block
Now, green is at [[38,10],[39,11]], which is in the narrow part of the container. I need to get it back to the portal position [[40,10],[41,11]] and try UP again.
But wait, earlier at step 5, UP from [[40,10],[41,11]] DID cause a swap. Let me check what was different then...
Actually, I just realized something. At step 5, the green pixel was at [[40,10],[41,11]] and after UP, it was at [[38,32],[39,33]]. But the divider block at [[38,32],[39,33]] is at a DIFFERENT row (38-39) than the portal (40-41). So the swap isn't with the divider at the same row, but with the divider block above.
Let me re-examine the divider blocks:
- Rows 10-11, 14-15, 18-19, 22-23, 26-27, 30-31, 34-35, 38-39, 42-43
The portal is at rows 40-41. The nearest divider blocks are:
- Above: rows 38-39
- Below: rows 42-43
So when green is at [[40,10],[41,11]] and presses UP, it swaps with the divider at [[38,32],[39,33]].
When green is at [[40,10],[41,11]] and presses DOWN, it swaps with the divider at [[42,32],[43,33]].
But now, when green is at [[40,10],[41,11]] and presses UP, it just moves to [[38,10],[39,11]] instead of swapping. Why?
OH! I think I see the issue. The swap only happens when there's NO container space in the direction of movement. When green is at [[40,10],[41,11]] and presses UP:
- If there's container space at [[38,10],[39,11]], green moves there (normal movement)
- If there's NO container space at [[38,10],[39,11]], green swaps with the divider
But the container DOES have space at [[38,10],[39,11]], so green just moves there.
So the question is: why did the swap happen at step 5? Was the container space at [[38,10],[39,11]] blocked then?
Let me check the ascii from step 5... I don't have it directly, but let me think about what happened before step 5.
Actually, I think the issue is that at step 5, the green pixel was at [[40,10],[41,11]] and the container at [[38,10],[39,11]] was occupied by something else (maybe another colored pixel or a gray block that was part of the container).
Wait, no. The container is gray, and green is a colored pixel inside it. The gray container blocks are the background, and green is the movable object. So green can move to any gray container cell.
Hmm, let me think about this differently. Maybe the "portal" swap is not about container space but about the specific position. Perhaps the swap only happens when green is at [[40,10],[41,11]] AND the divider block at [[38,32],[39,33]] is white (not already swapped).
But the divider at [[38,32],[39,33]] is still white now, so that's not the issue.
Actually, wait. Let me re-read the step 5 output. I don't have it directly, but from the world model:
"UP swaps active object with divider block above at same row level (VERIFIED step 5)"
So at step 5, green at [[40,10],[41,11]] swapped with the divider at [[38,32],[39,33]]. But now at step 22, green at [[40,10],[41,11]] just moved to [[38,10],[39,11]].
The key difference might be that at step 5, the container at [[38,10],[39,11]] was NOT available (maybe it was occupied by another object or was not part of the container). But now, it IS available.
Actually, I just realized something. The container layout might have changed! Earlier, when I first explored, the container might have had a different shape. Let me check the ascii from the initial state...
I don't have the initial ascii, but from the current ascii:
Row 38: ccggggNNccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
The 'g' is at positions 2-5 (cols 8-11), and 'N' is at positions 6-7 (cols 12-13). So the container is cols 8-11 at rows 38-39, and green is at cols 12-13.
But wait, if green is at cols 12-13 and the container is only cols 8-11, then green is outside the container. How can it move LEFT/RIGHT within the container?
I think the container actually extends to cols 12-13 at rows 38-39, but those cells are not rendered as 'g' because they're occupied by green. Let me check the ascii for rows 40-43:
Row 40: ccggggggccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
Row 41: ccggggggccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
At rows 40-41, the 'g' is at positions 2-7 (cols 8-13). So the container is cols 8-13 at rows 40-43.
So the container layout is:
- Rows 38-39: cols 8-13 (6 cols wide), but only cols 8-11 are rendered as 'g' and cols 12-13 are rendered as whatever is there (green 'N' or charcoal 'c')
- Rows 40-43: cols 8-13 (6 cols wide), all rendered as 'g'
This makes sense! The container is 6 cols wide (8-13) at all rows, but at rows 38-39, only the left part (8-11) is rendered as 'g', and the right part (12-13) is rendered as the background or the object inside.
So green can be at any position within cols 8-13, rows 38-43. The "portal" is at cols 10-11, rows 40-41.
Now, the question is: why did UP from [[40,10],[41,11]] cause a swap at step 5 but not at step 22?
I think the answer is that the swap happens when green is at the "right edge" of the narrow part (cols 10-11) and tries to go up, but the narrow part above (rows 38-39, cols 10-11) is "blocked" or "occupied" in some way.
But at step 22, green moved from [[40,10],[41,11]] to [[38,10],[39,11]], which means the space was available.
Hmm, maybe the swap only happens once per divider block? Like, once a divider block has been swapped, it can't be swapped again?
But the divider at [[38,32],[39,33]] is still white, so it hasn't been swapped.
Wait, let me check the node IDs. Earlier, the divider at [[38,32],[39,33]] had id=34. Now it still has id=34. So it's the same object.
But earlier, when the swap happened, the green pixel (id=34) moved to [[38,32],[39,33]] and became white, while the white divider (id=33) moved to [[40,10],[41,11]] and became green. So the IDs swapped.
Now, the green pixel has id=33 and the divider has id=34. So the IDs are in their original positions.
Hmm, but the swap should still work regardless of IDs. The mechanic is about positions and colors, not IDs.
Let me try a different approach. Let me check if the container at [[38,10],[39,11]] is actually part of the container or if it's just background. If it's background, then green moving there means it's leaving the container, which might trigger the swap.
Actually, I think I need to just try DOWN from the current position and see what happens. If green is at [[38,10],[39,11]], pressing DOWN should move it to [[40,10],[41,11]] (the portal position). Then I can try UP again.
But wait, I already tried UP from [[40,10],[41,11]] and it just moved to [[38,10],[39,11]]. So the swap doesn't seem to be working anymore.
Let me think about what's different. Maybe the swap only works when the green pixel is at the "bottom" of the container (rows 40-41) and the space above (rows 38-39) is NOT part of the container. But the container does include rows 38-39, cols 8-11.
Actually, I wonder if the container at rows 38-39, cols 10-11 is "blocked" by something. Let me check the ascii:
Row 38: ccggggNNccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
At positions 4-5 (cols 10-11), the characters are 'g' (gray). So the container is there. But at positions 6-7 (cols 12-13), the characters are 'N' (green). So green is at cols 12-13, not cols 10-11.
Wait, but the output says green is at [[38,10],[39,11]], which is cols 10-11. But the ascii shows 'N' at positions 6-7, which is cols 12-13 (since the substring starts at col 6).
Hmm, there's a discrepancy. Let me recalculate:
- The substring is lines[i][6:40], so position 0 in substring = col 6 in grid
- 'N' at positions 6-7 in substring = cols 12-13 in grid
But the segmentation says green is at [[38,10],[39,11]], which is cols 10-11.
Wait, maybe I'm miscounting the ascii. Let me look at the actual ascii output again:
"ccggggNNccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB"
Let me count character by character:
c(0) c(1) g(2) g(3) g(4) g(5) N(6) N(7) c(8) c(9) c(10) c(11) c(12) c(13) c(14) c(15) c(16) c(17) c(18) c(19) c(20) c(21) c(22) c(23) c(24) c(25) c(26) c(27) c(28) c(29) W(30) W(31) B(32) B(33) B(34) B(35) B(36) B(37) B(38) B(39)
So 'N' is at substring positions 6-7, which means grid cols 6+6=12 and 6+7=13. So green is at cols 12-13.
But the segmentation says green is at [[38,10],[39,11]], which is cols 10-11.
There's a discrepancy! Either the ascii or the segmentation is wrong, or I'm miscounting.
Actually, wait. The ascii output I'm looking at might be from a different crop. Let me check the code that produced the ascii:
```
print(f"Row {i}: {lines[i][28:42]}")
```
Oh! The ascii output is lines[i][28:42], not lines[i][6:40]. So the substring starts at col 28, not col 6.
So 'N' at substring positions 6-7 means grid cols 28+6=34 and 28+7=35. But that's in the right panel, not the left panel where green should be.
Hmm, that doesn't make sense either. Let me look at the ascii output again:
"Row 38: ccccWWBBBBBBBW"
Wait, this is from the divider area crop (cols 28-42). The 'W' at positions 4-5 means grid cols 28+4=32 and 28+5=33, which is the divider. That makes sense.
But where is green in this crop? Green is at cols 10-11, which is outside the crop range (28-42). So green doesn't appear in this ascii output.
OK so I was looking at the wrong ascii. Let me focus on the segmentation data, which says green is at [[38,10],[39,11]].
So green is at rows 38-39, cols 10-11. This is in the narrow part of the container. The portal is at rows 40-41, cols 10-11.
To trigger the portal swap, I need to:
1. Move green DOWN to [[40,10],[41,11]] (portal position)
2. Press UP to swap with divider
But I already tried this and UP just moved green back to [[38,10],[39,11]] instead of swapping.
Let me try a different approach. Maybe the portal swap only works when green is at the "bottom" of the container and tries to go DOWN, not UP. Let me try DOWN from the portal position.
Actually, from the world model: "DOWN: Swaps active object back with divider block, reversing UP (VERIFIED step 6)"
So DOWN from the portal position should also swap with the divider (the one below at rows 42-43).
Let me try: DOWN to get to portal, then DOWN again to swap with the divider below.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Green at [[38,10],[39,11]]. UP from portal [[40,10],[41,11]] just moves up, doesn't swap.
# Hypothesis: portal swap only works in one direction or under specific conditions.
# Try DOWN from portal to swap with divider below at [[42,32],[43,33]]

# First get to portal position
action(['DOWN'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green after DOWN: {n['boundary']}")

# Now at portal [[40,10],[41,11]], try DOWN to swap with divider below
action(['DOWN'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"\nAfter DOWN from portal:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green: id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        b = n['boundary']
        if 36 <= b[0][0] <= 44:
            print(f"White: id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

print(f"board_changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves active object right 2px within container (VERIFIED)", "LEFT": "Moves active object left 2px within container (VERIFIED)", "UP": "At portal (cols 10-11, rows 40-41): may swap with divider above or just move up (UNCERTAIN)", "DOWN": "Moves down within container, may swap at portal (VERIFIED)", "MOUSE": "Does NOT change active object (VERIFIED)"}, "object_taxonomy": {"green_pixel": "Active player object, 2x2", "divider_blocks": "White 2x2 blocks at col 32-33"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "White bar at row 63"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Green at [[38,10],[39,11]]. Portal at rows 40-41, cols 10-11. sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Green at [[38,10],[39,11]]. UP from portal [[40,10],[41,11]] just moves up, doesn't swap.
# Hypothesis: portal swap only works in one direction or under specific conditions.
# Try DOWN from portal to swap with divider below at [[42,32],[43,33]]

# First get to portal position
action(['DOWN'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green after DOWN: {n['boundary']}")

# Now at portal [[40,10],[41,11]], try DOWN to swap with divider below
action(['DOWN'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"\nAfter DOWN from portal:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green: id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        b = n['boundary']
        if 36 <= b[0][0] <= 44:
            print(f"White: id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

print(f"board_changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
